data.title: Write RED test: Downstream Consumers section structural boundary
data.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## Overview
Write a failing structural boundary test for the ## Downstream Consumers section in implementation-plan SKILL.md, following the behavioral-testing-standard.md Rule 5 pattern (test the structural boundary of instruction files, not content).

## Test File
tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh

## Given / When / Then Test Cases

### test_downstream_consumers_heading_present
Given: plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md
When: searching for a ## Downstream Consumers section heading in Step 3
Then: FAIL (section does not yet exist before implementation)

### test_downstream_consumers_distinct_from_file_impact
Given: plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md
When: checking that Downstream Consumers is a separate ## heading, not nested under ## File Impact
Then: FAIL (section not present)

### test_downstream_consumers_omit_rule_present
Given: plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md
When: searching for the "when no consumers found, section is omitted" rule text
Then: FAIL (rule not yet present)

## Implementation Notes
- Follow the pattern from tests/scripts/test-implementation-plan-task-description.sh
- Use awk range extraction to scope checks to the relevant section
- Set executable bit (chmod +x) after creating the file
- Add .test-index RED marker [test_downstream_consumers_heading_present] for plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) after implementation task completes
- [ ] ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py passes (exit 0)
- [ ] Test file exists at tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh
  Verify: test -f tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh
- [ ] Test file is executable
  Verify: test -x tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh
- [ ] All 3 test functions present in the test file
  Verify: grep -c "^test_downstream_consumers" tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh | grep -q "^3"
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker [test_downstream_consumers_heading_present] for plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md
  Verify: grep '\[test_downstream_consumers_heading_present\]' .test-index
- [ ] .test-index entry maps plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md to tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers.sh
  Verify: grep 'test-implementation-plan-downstream-consumers' .test-index
